div.head: The Ambassador in France (Edge) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 54. Department’s telegram No. 29, February 6, 5 p.m. As BoncourJoseph Paul-Boncour, French Minister for Foreign Affairs. and MassigliRené Massigli, Chief, League of Nations Section, French Foreign Office. are both in Geneva I repeated your telegram to the American Delegation at Geneva and Wilson will take matter up with Boncour this afternoon.

In addition MarrinerJames Theodore Marriner, Counselor of Embassy in France. called on LegerAlexis Leger, Vice Political Director, French Foreign Office. who said that there was no question of France’s attitude on the subject and the necessary instruction went forward to Lima yesterday calling Peru’s attention to her obligations under the Briand-Kellogg Pact. They did not specifically support the Brazilian proposal as it was felt that the method of solution was principally a question for the American continents. Word of this action has been sent to ClaudelPaul Claudel, French Ambassador in the United States. today.
